---
title: '[WIP]Uniswap vs. CEXs'
description: ''
date: '2023-07-01'
categories: [analysis, crypto]
image: 'preview.png'
---

In this post, I will compare crypto trading activities on Uniswap to those on Centralized Exchanges (CEXs). But first, let's have a quick lookback on some of key events in crypto exchange history.

On Jan 3, 2009, Satoshi Nakamoto mined the first block of Bitcoin.

One year later, on Mar 17, 2010, Bitcoinmarket.com, the first crypto exchange went live. [link](https://www.cryptohopper.com/blog/what-was-the-first-crypto-exchange-449)

Four months later, on Jul 18, 2010, mtgox.com, the famous defunct exchange, launched. By Apr 2013, and into early 2014, it became the largest Bitcoin exchange, handling over 70% of total Bitcoin trades. On Feb 28, 2014, after a series of major security breaches, and bitcoin stolens, the exchange filed for bankruptcy. [link](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mt._Gox)

Meanwhile, in Jun 2012, Brian Amstrong founded Coinbase, the current largest CEX in the US. [link](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coinbase)

Six months later, Bitfinex was founded. [link](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bitfinex)

Five years after the launch of Coinbase, on Jul 14, 2017, Changpeng Zhao founded Binance, the current largest CEX in the world. [link](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Binance)

In the same year, OKX was launched. [link](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/OKX)

Over a year later, on Nov 2, 2018, Hayden Adams founded Uniswap, the first, and also the current largest DEX in the world. [link](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Uniswap)

It's been 14 years since the Bitcoin's genesis block mined, 13 years since the first CEX founded, and almost 4 years since the first DEX founded. With 8 years lesser in operation, how has DEX performed vs. CEX? And with 1 years to 6 years lesser in operation, how has Uniswap performed vs. Binance, Coinbase, OKX, and Bitfinex? Let's find out!

::: {.callout-tip}
## How to read charts

-   Charts from theblockcrypto.com has default zoom of the last 12 months. There are other options at the bottom right corner of the charts. Some comments below are of all time zoom option, so if you want to see related data, click the zoom **all** option.

-   Sometimes, if the charts from theblockcrypto.com don't show up due to embeded error, please click their source links located right below them.
:::

# DEX vs. CEX

To service a trade, there must be liquidity. First, let's examine liquidity.

## Liquidity

*In this section, let's look at total asset deposited in DEX (i.e. TVL, value locked), and in CEX (i.e. [proof of reserves](https://www.coingecko.com/learn/what-is-proof-of-reserves-por)).*

<!-- prepare for plotly charts -->

```{python}
import requests
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.graph_objs as go
import plotly.io as pio
from datetime import datetime

pd.options.mode.chained_assignment = None  # default='warn'

# set plotly default theme
pio.templates.default = 'plotly_white'
```

<!-- get CEX proof of reserves -->

```{python}
r = requests.get('https://api.llama.fi/protocols')
```

```{python}
df = pd.DataFrame(r.json())
df_essential = df[['name','category','slug','tvl','parentProtocol','twitter']]
df_exchange = df_essential.query('category in ("CEX","Dexes")')

# extract the part after 'parent#' using str.extract() method
df_exchange['parentName'] = df_exchange['parentProtocol'].str.extract(r'parent#(.*)')

df_exchange['parentName'] = df_exchange['parentName'].str.capitalize()

df_exchange['parentName'] = df_exchange['parentName'].fillna(df_exchange['name'])
```

Total DEX assets is roughly 15% of total CEX assets.

```{python}
df_cat = df_exchange[['category','tvl']].groupby(by='category', as_index=False).sum()

fig = px.bar(
    df_cat, x='category', y='tvl',
    title='Exchange Total Assets',
    text_auto='.4s'
)

fig.update_traces(
    textposition="outside",
    textfont_size=12, textangle=0, cliponaxis=False
)

fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title='Exchange Category', 
    yaxis_title='Total Assets in USD'
)

fig.show()
```

There are 839 DEXs tracked on Defillama. Top 20 DEXs account for 60% of total DEX assets. Uniswap is the largest DEX in total assets.

```{python}
df_dex = df_exchange.query('category == "Dexes"')

df_dex_agg = df_dex[['parentName', 'category', 'tvl']].groupby(by=['parentName', 'category'], as_index=False).sum()

df_dex_agg = df_dex_agg.sort_values('tvl', ascending=False)

df_top20 = df_dex_agg.iloc[:20]

rest_count = df_dex_agg['tvl'].iloc[20:].count()

df_rest = pd.DataFrame({'parentName': [f'Other {rest_count} protocols'], 'tvl': [df_dex_agg['tvl'].iloc[20:].sum()]})

df_top20_rest = pd.concat([df_rest, df_top20.iloc[::-1]])

fig = px.bar(
    df_top20_rest, x='tvl', y='parentName',
    title='DEX Total Assets',
    text_auto='.3s',
    height=800
)

fig.update_traces(
    textposition="outside",
)

fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title='Total Assets in USD', 
    yaxis_title='DEX'
)

fig.show()
```

There are 28 CEXs tracked on Defillama. Top 3 CEXs account for almost 80% of total CEX assets. Binance is the largest CEX in total assets.

```{python}
df_cex = df_exchange.query('category == "CEX"').sort_values('tvl', ascending=False).iloc[::-1]

fig = px.bar(
    df_cex, x='tvl', y='name',
    title='CEX Total Assets',
    text_auto='.3s',
    height=800
)

fig.update_traces(
    textposition="outside",
)

fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title='Total Assets in USD', 
    yaxis_title='CEX'
)

fig.show()
```

In top 10 exchanges, only 2 of them are DEXs, which are Uniswap, and Curve, at 4th and 5th position respectively.

```{python}
df_cex1 = df_cex[['parentName', 'category', 'tvl']]
df_exchange_adj = pd.concat([df_cex1, df_dex_agg])
n = 10
df_top_ex = df_exchange_adj.sort_values('tvl', ascending=False).iloc[:n]

#reverse to show in descending order of tvl
df_top_ex = df_top_ex.iloc[::-1]

fig = px.bar(
    df_top_ex, x='tvl', y='parentName',
    title=f'Top {n} Exchange Total Assets',
    text_auto='.3s'
)

fig.update_traces(
    textposition="outside",
)

fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title='Total Assets in USD', 
    yaxis_title='Exchange'
)

fig.show()
```

## Trades

*In this section, to compare DEX to CEX, let's look at DEX to CEX spot volume, which is monthly decentralized exchange volume divided by centralized exchange volume (as a percentage).*

One and a half year after the first data point in Jan 2019, DEX to CEX spot volume first surpassed 1% in Jun 2020. Four months later, DEX to CEX spot volume skyrocketed to 16% in Sep 2020. Sixteen months later, it made a higher high to 17% in Jan 2022.

And recently, in May 2023, it made all time high at 22%. That marked DEX spot volume surpassed a fifth of that on CEX.

<iframe width="100%" height="420" frameborder="0" src="https://embed.theblockcrypto.com/data/decentralized-finance/dex-non-custodial/dex-to-cex-spot-trade-volume/embed" title="DEX to CEX Spot Trade Volume (%)"></iframe>

*source: [theblockcrypto.com](https://www.theblockcrypto.com/data/decentralized-finance/dex-non-custodial/dex-to-cex-spot-trade-volume).*

In absolute $, spot volume of DEX and CEX's all time high were $213b and $4.25t, both recorded in May 2021, which translates to 9% of DEX to CEX spot volume.

Last 12 months, though both spot volume on DEX and CEX have generally been down, DEX to CEX spot volume (as seen above) has been up, which indicates a slower drop in DEX spot volume.

<iframe width="100%" height="420" frameborder="0" src="https://embed.theblockcrypto.com/data/decentralized-finance/dex-non-custodial/dex-volume-monthly/embed" title="DEX Volume"></iframe>

*source: [theblockcrypto.com](https://www.theblockcrypto.com/data/decentralized-finance/dex-non-custodial/dex-volume-monthly).*

<iframe width="100%" height="420" frameborder="0" src="https://embed.theblockcrypto.com/data/crypto-markets/spot/cryptocurrency-exchange-volume-monthly/embed" title="Cryptocurrency Monthly Exchange Volume"></iframe>

*source: [theblockcrypto.com](https://www.theblockcrypto.com/data/crypto-markets/spot/cryptocurrency-exchange-volume-monthly).*

Dominances of Uniswap - the largest DEX, and Binance - the largest CEX, in each category are quite similar (40%-60% market share).

Overall, market share of Binance has been in a steeper upward slope than that of Uniswap.

Last 12 months, market shares of top 2-5 CEXs are more evenly distributed than those of top 2-5 DEXs.

<iframe width="100%" height="420" frameborder="0" src="https://embed.theblockcrypto.com/data/decentralized-finance/dex-non-custodial/share-of-dex-volume-monthly/embed" title="Share of DEX Volume"></iframe>

*source: [theblockcrypto.com](https://www.theblockcrypto.com/data/decentralized-finance/dex-non-custodial/share-of-dex-volume-monthly).*

<iframe width="100%" height="420" frameborder="0" src="https://embed.theblockcrypto.com/data/crypto-markets/spot/the-block-legitimate-index-market-share/embed" title="Monthly Exchange Volume Market Share"></iframe>

*source: [theblockcrypto.com](https://www.theblockcrypto.com/data/crypto-markets/spot/the-block-legitimate-index-market-share).*

# Uniswap vs. Top CEXs

*In this section, depends on data availability, I will compare Uniswap to at least 1 CEX among Binance, Coinbase, OKX, and Bitfinex.*

## Trades

<iframe width="100%" height="420" frameborder="0" src="https://embed.theblockcrypto.com/data/decentralized-finance/dex-non-custodial/uniswap-vs-coinbase-and-binance-trade-volume-7dma/embed" title="Uniswap vs. Coinbase and Binance Trade Volume (7DMA)"></iframe>

<iframe src="https://dune.com/embeds/2658402/4419143" width="100%" height="400"></iframe>
